title: GET /og/ping — diagnostic probe for the OG renderer
problem: When /og/share/:id or /og/home misbehaves, the FIRST question is 'is the functions/og/ tree even being picked up at deploy?'. A 404 there could mean Pages dropped the bundle, the bundle size exceeded the limit, or _routes.json drifted. /og/ping is the answer: plain-text 'og-ok' if reachable, JSON probe of font asset state if requested. Smaller, faster, and disjoint from the satori render path.
analysis: GET only. Default returns text/plain 'og-ok' with cache-control no-store (so a probe always hits the function). ?fonts=1 returns JSON: a per-font load result (ok + size + content-type per file in functions/_lib/og-fonts.ts's probe list). Returns 200 if all fonts load, 503 if any failed. Used during deploy verification and RUNBOOK § OG triage to isolate 'the function is reachable' from 'the render pipeline works'.

decisions.decision: Plain text 'og-ok' as the default response, not JSON
decisions.rationale: Plain text is the smallest possible signal that the function is alive. JSON or HTML would imply we're testing something else (parser, framework). The ASCII string is unambiguous — a curl pipe to grep just works.
decisions.recommendation: Don't elaborate the default response. If new diagnostics are needed, add another ?flag mode.

decisions.rationale: Font probe loads every TTF/OTF, which takes ~50ms cold. Doing it on every /og/ping would slow down the basic reachability check. Opt-in via the query param keeps the basic probe instant and surfaces detailed font state only when asked.
decisions.recommendation: When adding another probe mode (e.g. ?cache=1), follow the same opt-in pattern.

successCriteria:
- /og/ping returns 200 + 'og-ok' as plain text
- /og/ping?fonts=1 returns 200 + JSON when all fonts load; 503 when any fail
- Cache-control is no-store so probes always reach the function
- Disjoint from the satori render path — works even when satori is broken
implementation: functions/og/ping.ts — single onRequestGet. probeOgFonts (functions/_lib/og-fonts.ts) handles the per-font load + JSON projection. The default response is hardcoded; minimal allocations.
userGuide: Used during deploy verification: curl https://marcportal.com/og/ping should return 'og-ok'. If it returns 404 or HTML, Pages dropped the functions/og/ tree — check bundle size in deploy logs. If it returns 'og-ok' but /og/share/:id is broken, the satori path is the problem; check ?fonts=1. RUNBOOK § OG documents the full triage tree.
knownLimitations: ?fonts=1 loads all fonts; ~50ms cold. Not suitable for high-frequency uptime monitoring
Doesn't probe the satori render itself — that's intentional, would require rendering a test image which has its own failure surface
If the worker is broken before reaching this handler (e.g. cold-start crash), the response will be CF's generic 5xx page rather than 'og-ok'; that's still useful signal

statusHistory.reason: Diagnostic endpoint shipped alongside the OG renderer.
Code-first doc co-located. Two-mode probe stable; RUNBOOK depends on it.
